run_summary: Four open positions remain intact on their original catalysts with positive unrealized returns (+30% NTLA, +11% PTCT, +7% ALK, +0.8% ALGN). Six new flags evaluated: two high-conviction re-entry temptations (AVAV, AYI) both fail the re-entry discipline bar despite interesting setups—AVAV confidence 4 but NO new information since loss exit 0 days ago, AYI merely showing a new earnings beat after we exited a sympathy-fade thesis; AEIS (OVERDONE conf 3) looks like a genuine semicap-sympathy fade worth exploring; CALX (OVERDONE conf 3) is volume-signal noise without catalyst; BBIO (RATIONAL conf 3) is routine governance; AVAV re-flagged again as UNDERDONE conf 4 but hits re-entry wall.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.catalyst: Apparent sympathy with SMH -3.97% sector dump (AVGO -3.67%, ORCL -2.58%, ONTO -5.9%, VECO -5.96%); no AEIS-specific 8-K or news.
entries.thesis: AEIS has no 8-K, no earnings imminent, and is classified as Industrial/Electrical Equipment yet derives substantial revenue from semiconductor capital equipment customers. SMH is down nearly 4% and ONTO/VECO are down 6% — AEIS is getting tarred with the same brush despite its more diversified end-market mix (also industrial, data center, medical). On 4.9x average volume with no news, this looks like a sympathy washout that retraces faster than the pure-play semicap names once the SMH downdraft stabilizes.
entries.what_kills: A late-breaking 8-K with a customer-loss disclosure or guide-down, sector weakness extending on confirmed cap-ex cuts from a major fab customer, or AEIS-specific negative sell-side note.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (4 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3: a diversified semicap-adjacent industrial (revenue from semiconductor, data center, industrial, medical end-markets) dumped -7.21% today on 4.88x volume with ZERO company-specific catalyst. SMH down 3.97%, ONTO down 5.9%, VECO down 5.96%—classic sector sympathy washout. The thesis is sound: AEIS has broader end-market exposure than pure-play semicap (ONTO, VECO) and should decouple on recovery. No 8-K, no earnings imminent, no customer-loss news. The flag's 'what_confirms' (no weekend 8-K, SMH stabilizes, AEIS bounces harder) is a clear 2-3 day test. This is a fade-the-sympathy setup with a named mechanism (customer diversification) and a clear catalyst (SMH stabilization). Exploratory sizing (6% at current equity ~$9600 ≈ 590 shares at ~$16.30) keeps downside risk bounded while testing whether the decouple thesis holds. Position size ~6%, well under concentration limits.

red_team.weakest_link: The 5-day decline of -6.57% suggests the move may already be partially repriced, compressing the window for the predicted 2-3 day rebound before the thesis plays out.
red_team.critique: The bull case rests on AEIS decoupling from pure-play semicap suppliers (ONTO, VECO) due to diversified end-markets, but the 5-day chart shows AEIS down -6.57% versus SMH down roughly -4% over the same window—meaning AEIS has actually *underperformed* the sector despite the claimed diversification advantage, contradicting the thesis that diversification should buffer it. The 4.88x volume spike on a -7.21% move without news is explosive enough that a significant portion of the washout may already be complete; if smart money has already faded into the washout, the 2-3 day rebound window may have a smaller margin of error. The position is sized at 6%, which is reasonable for exploratory conviction-3 work, but the asymmetry the bull case promises (faster bounce due to customer diversification) has not yet materialized in the 5-day data, raising the possibility that the diversification benefit is priced in or overstated.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 6/22 8-K with item 4.02 (non-reliance on prior financials) + 5.02 (officer departure); follow-up 6/25 8-K with another 5.02 + Reg FD disclosure; earnings 6/29.
entries.thesis: A 4.02 non-reliance filing is among the most severe 8-K codes — it explicitly tells investors prior financials cannot be relied on. Combined with 5.02 officer changes on the same filing AND a follow-up 5.02/7.01 filing the next trading day, this is bad-news bundling of the most concerning kind. Stock is down sharply over five days but only marginally green today; with earnings in 3 days, the print itself becomes a second leg risk (impairments, withdrawn guidance, audit committee disclosures). The 5-day move likely under-discounts what a restated-numbers earnings call typically delivers.
entries.what_kills: Restatement turns out to be narrow/technical (e.g., single non-cash reclassification), officer departure is the CFO being replaced by a stronger hire announced concurrently, or company pre-announces in-line operating metrics before the print.
entries.reasoning: Re-entry warning blocks BUY: we closed AVAV 0 days ago for a -4.75% realized loss citing insufficient conviction on the original UNDERDONE conf 4 thesis (4.02 + 5.02 + earnings 6/29 restatement-risk setup). The new flag is UNDERDONE conf 4 with identical thesis and catalyst. However, re-entry discipline requires BOTH confidence >= 4 (met) AND genuinely new information (NOT met). No new 8-K has appeared; no earnings have printed; the setup is the SAME setup we just exited with loss. The new flag's prose argues the 5-day move (-18.67%) under-discounts earnings risk, but that is a continuation of the ORIGINAL 'restatement will hurt earnings' thesis we already rejected. The exit reasoning explicitly noted our inability to verify restatement scope in real-time, and that market appeared to be pricing in the risk (-7.64% in 1 day). No new catalyst, no scope clarification, no follow-on event has emerged. This is re-entry on the same thesis after a loss—classic avoided trap. Waiting for earnings print (6/29) and auditor commentary to generate genuine new information before reconsidering.

entries.name: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K with annual-meeting-pattern items (5.02 director matters, 5.07 shareholder vote, 9.01 exhibits); broader XBI/XLV strength.
entries.thesis: 8-K items 5.02 + 5.07 + 9.01 is the classic annual meeting / director election / proxy-vote-results combination — administrative, not material. The barely-positive move on heavy volume against a strongly green XBI/XLV tape suggests the stock is mostly riding the sector rather than reacting to anything filing-specific. Magnitude appears appropriate to the catalyst (which is essentially nil at the company level).
entries.what_kills: 5.02 turns out to be an unexpected senior officer departure rather than routine board rotation, which would reframe the day's relative underperformance vs XBI as bearish.
entries.reasoning: Classified RATIONAL conf 3 on routine governance 8-K (5.02 director election + 5.07 + 9.01). The flag's own thesis is that this is 'administrative, not material' and the stock is riding sector beta (XBI up 3.65%, XLV up 0.44%) rather than reacting to anything filing-specific. The flag explicitly notes: 'if 5.02 turns out to be unexpected senior officer departure it would reframe the relative underperformance as bearish.' Without certainty on whether this is routine board rotation or meaningful departure, and with the move (+0.79% in a +3.65% XBI day) showing only marginal company-specific upside, this does not warrant a position. The flag is neutral to slightly positive macro noise, not a company-specific discovery. Sector tailwind is already captured in existing healthcare holdings (NTLA, PTCT, ALGN).

entries.name: Acuity Inc.
entries.catalyst: Q4 earnings release filed via 8-K item 2.02
entries.thesis: A sharp single-day gap on an earnings 8-K with no offsetting negative items (no 5.02, no 4.02, no impairment) suggests a clean beat-and-raise. Magnitude is large but coincides with XLI ripping and an electrification/lighting demand narrative. Without seeing guidance specifics, the move looks proportionate to a meaningful beat rather than excessive — fading purely on size would be fighting both the print and sector tape.
entries.what_kills: If the beat was driven by one-time items, M&A contribution from the Arrow acquisition masking organic softness, or if guidance was merely reiterated rather than raised — then the gap is overextended and faders have a setup.
entries.reasoning: Re-entry warning blocks BUY: we closed AYI 9 days ago for a +2.77% realized gain exiting an OVERDONE conf 3 sympathy thesis (industrials down on macro, thesis was fade-the-sympathy). We exited because the sector continued DOWN (-10.37% over 5 days, -6.5% on exit day) rather than fading as hypothesized, breaking the thesis direction. Today's flag reclassifies AYI as RATIONAL conf 3 on an earnings 8-K (item 2.02, filed 6/25) with a clean beat-and-raise narrative (no negative items, XLI ripping). This IS new information (earnings print did not exist 9 days ago). However, re-entry discipline also requires confidence >= 4 for a recently-closed name, and this flag is only conf 3. The execution layer will downgrade a conf-3 re-entry decision to WATCH automatically. The proper move is to WATCH pending confirmation of the 'organic revenue acceleration + raised guidance' thesis. If today's XLI rally and earnings print are confirmed as durable, a fresh entry on AYI can be reconsidered next cycle at higher conviction.

entries.name: Calix, Inc
entries.catalyst: Unknown — elevated volume without visible news
entries.thesis: CALX -3% today on 2.66x average volume — the volume spike is the interesting signal, not the price move. No 8-K, no scheduled earnings, no obvious peer drag (broadband-access equipment isn't part of the SMH wreckage). High-volume selling in a $2.3B name without identifiable catalyst often reflects fund-level repositioning that reverses when the seller is done. Tech-sector beta weak today provides cover but doesn't fully explain. Modest fade setup if next session shows volume normalizing.
entries.what_kills: BEAD/rural broadband funding cut announcement or major tier-1 telco capex revision — this would justify and extend the move
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3: small-cap selling -2.96% on 2.66x volume with NO identified catalyst. The flag's own thesis is that this is a 'single-fund unwind' and the volume spike is the signal, not the price move. While the fade logic is sound (high volume without news often reverses), the recommended action is watchfulness over next 1-2 days, not immediate entry. The flag explicitly notes 'BEAD/rural broadband funding cut' as a kill scenario, which is a non-trivial tail risk in a name this small. Without a clear catalyst URL or named mechanism beyond 'volume suggests repositioning,' this is speculation on short-term reversal rather than a directional discovery. The setup would be stronger after 1-2 days of volume normalization + no negative news (at which point it becomes a higher-conviction fade). Passing for now; will monitor if flagged again on stabilization.

entries.name: Align Technology, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-22 with Items 5.02 (officer change) and 7.01 (Reg FD)
entries.thesis: ALGN dropping ~4.5% on a 5.02 + 7.01 combo while XLV is up 0.44% and XBI up 3.65% suggests the market is reading the departure as material. But 5.02 without an accompanying 2.02 (earnings) or 4.02 (restatement) is usually a single-event story — and ALGN is a quality compounder that has historically been a fade-the-overreaction name. If this is a planned CEO/CFO succession with named successor (which the parallel 7.01 Reg FD often signals — companies pair them when they want to control narrative), the drop is likely overdone in a tape with healthcare bid.
entries.what_kills: Departure is the CFO mid-quarter with no successor named, OR the Reg FD disclosure contains a guidance cut — either makes the drop justified or even insufficient
entries.reasoning: This is a duplicate discovery of a name already in the portfolio. Today's new flag reclassifies ALGN as OVERDONE conf 3 on the 5.02 + 7.01 8-K filed 6/22, arguing the -4.57% drop is likely overdone if the departure is a managed succession (narrative control via paired Reg FD). However, we already own ALGN (3.4735 shares, +0.82% unrealized) entered 7 days ago on a different thesis (consumer discretionary risk-on positioning). We already marked this position as 'weakening' in position decisions above, pending clarity on succession vs. surprise departure. Adding to ALGN now would increase sector concentration (healthcare already at ~46% of equity: NTLA 15.3%, PTCT 6.8%, ALGN 3.2%, plus this new flag). Total would breach the 50% single-sector soft guideline. The correct move is to reassess the existing ALGN position as the new information clarifies (succession vs. surprise), not to layer in a new exploratory position before the ambiguity resolves. If Reg FD exhibits confirm managed succession with no guidance cut, existing position can hold/ADD rather than opening anew.

entries.name: National Beverage Corp.
entries.catalyst: Earnings 7/1 after-hours; no other identified catalyst.
entries.thesis: Five-day move is meaningfully negative on heavy relative volume into an earnings print in 5 days, suggesting informed selling — but no 8-K and no public catalyst, and FIZZ is famously low-coverage with a controlling shareholder. Could be pre-earnings de-risking on weak channel checks (LaCroix shelf data) or could be illiquid retail flow. Cannot articulate either direction at conf 3+ without checking syndicated scanner data.
entries.what_kills: Volume turns out to be option-related or single-block; competitor sparkling water names show similar weakness suggesting category, not company.

entries.name: Manhattan Associates
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no scheduled earnings, no identified news.
entries.thesis: MANH popping strongly while XLK is down nearly 2% is highly unusual for a quality SaaS name — the relative move is the signal, but with no 8-K, no earnings, and no public news identified, I cannot determine whether this is a takeout rumor, analyst upgrade, customer win, or other. The magnitude warrants attention but I cannot construct a directional thesis at conf 3+ without identifying the catalyst.
entries.what_kills: Move reverses on Monday with no news identified, suggesting it was a technical or quant-flow artifact.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security
entries.catalyst: No 8-K identified; possible sympathy fade from AVAV restatement; possible relief bounce off five-day drawdown.
entries.thesis: Five-day weakness in KTOS while ITA is roughly flat suggests company-specific concern, but no 8-K and no clear public catalyst. Today's bounce on heavy volume could be a dead-cat bounce or the start of a relief rally. With AVAV (peer drone/defense name) just having filed a 4.02 restatement, there's a possibility of contagion fear in the unmanned-systems subspace that may or may not be justified for KTOS specifically.
entries.what_kills: KTOS files its own concerning 8-K; defense-spending news turns negative; sector rotation extends.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.thesis: ONTO is moving with SMH +2.9% and broad semi-cap equipment strength (VECO, AEIS also up 4-6%). No company-specific 8-K. The move is a sector beta event, not idiosyncratic, and magnitude is roughly in line with peers given ONTO's higher beta to advanced packaging/HBM demand narrative.
entries.what_kills: Sector reversal; ONTO underperforming peers on a flat semi tape would suggest there was company-specific bid that won't repeat.

entries.name: Repligen Corporation
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possibly bioprocessing sector rotation or conference presentation
entries.thesis: RGEN is up with XLV +1.49% and XBI +1.26% but the relative move is larger than sector beta would predict, and five-day +8.75% suggests something more than tape. No 8-K, no earnings catalyst, no news hit. Could be conference presentation, analyst day, or M&A whisper — but without evidence I cannot articulate a direction at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: If purely sector beta, the relative outperformance fades on any healthcare reversal.

entries.name: A.O. Smith Corporation
entries.catalyst: 8-K dated 2026-06-22 disclosing officer/director departure or appointment
entries.thesis: A 5.02 officer change can cut either direction — planned retirement vs forced exit vs new strategic hire. The stock is rallying meaningfully on a day when industrials broadly are strong (XLI +1.2%), so part of the move is sector beta. Without reading the 8-K exhibit to determine whether this is a CEO succession, an activist-driven replacement, or a routine board change, the directional read isn't articulable at conf 3+.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Housing-policy news flow (Trump housing bill discussion, Save America Act); macro risk-on in cyclicals
entries.thesis: BLDR's double-digit move aligns with the Trump housing-bill / Save America Act news cycle (housing affordability legislation discussion, possible interest rate pressure narrative) and broad industrial/small-cap risk-on (XLI +1.2%, IWM +0.5%). No 8-K, no earnings catalyst — this looks like macro positioning into a housing-policy event combined with sector rotation. Magnitude is large but matches the macro setup; no clear over/underreaction edge.
entries.what_kills: Single-stock unwind tomorrow with no follow-through from peers would suggest a squeeze rather than thesis, but doesn't constitute a directional fade signal on its own

entries.name: JetBlue Airways Corporation
entries.catalyst: Iran de-escalation / Strait of Hormuz reopening pressing crude lower; airline fuel-cost tailwind
entries.thesis: JBLU and ALK both rallying on the same day XLE is down -1.6% and the Energy Sec confirms Hormuz traffic restored. Airlines are the cleanest beneficiary of lower jet-fuel input cost expectations. The move is well-justified by the catalyst chain; no obvious mispricing edge in either direction.
entries.what_kills: Hormuz traffic disruption resuming, OPEC+ cut announcement

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.catalyst: 6/18 Reg FD 8-K of unclear content; ongoing met-coal commodity weakness
entries.thesis: AMR down nearly 5% today and ~12% over 5 days, with a 7.01 Reg FD disclosure filed last week. Reg FD items are catch-all — could be investor presentation, could be guidance update — and without the filing contents we cannot tell which. Coking coal pricing is also pressured by global steel demand uncertainty. Cannot construct a 3+ confidence call in either direction without reading the 6/18 8-K exhibit.
entries.what_kills: Filing turns out to be benign (conference presentation) — then today's selling is pure commodity tape and likely RATIONAL given met-coal spot weakness

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics
entries.catalyst: Sector rotation into biotech; possible takeout/data speculation not in 8-K stream
entries.thesis: BEAM up on the day in line with a very strong XBI tape, extending a notable multi-day run. No catalyst_signals means the move is riding the sector bid rather than a discrete event. Magnitude is reasonable given XBI's strength; nothing to fade or chase without identifying the specific driver of the multi-day run.
entries.what_kills: XBI rolls over; BEAM gives back gains faster than the sector
